lab.name: Wild & Heritage Species
lab.description: Non-Saccharomyces and wild alcohol-producing microbes, catalogued at the species level. These entries describe the organism and its fermentation behaviour rather than a single commercial product; specs are ranges from research/literature and are null where no reliable figure exists.

attribution: Species descriptions and fermentation ranges compiled from published brewing/oenology literature; not a single vendor spec sheet. Individual commercial isolates of these species appear under their own labs.

strains.name: Brettanomyces bruxellensis (species)
strains.description: The classic 'Brett' of Belgian sour/wild ales and faulty wines. Slowly ferments dextrins Saccharomyces leaves behind, driving very high attenuation over months and producing barnyard/leather/tropical phenols and esters.

strains.name: Lachancea thermotolerans (species)
strains.description: A yeast that produces both lactic acid and ethanol, souring and fermenting in one step without bacteria. Increasingly used in wine to lower pH and in beer for bacteria-free sours (e.g. commercially as WildBrew Philly Sour).

strains.name: Torulaspora delbrueckii (species)
strains.description: A non-Saccharomyces yeast valued for clean, aromatic fermentation with low volatile acidity; used in co-fermentation for wine complexity and in saison/low-alcohol beer.

strains.name: Kluyveromyces marxianus (species)
strains.description: A fast-growing, highly thermotolerant yeast that can ferment lactose (unlike most brewing yeast), making it useful for whey/dairy-based and fruit ferments and high-temperature ethanol. Produces fruity esters; being explored for novel beverages.
strains.flavorNotes: Fruity, ester-rich; can ferment milk sugar.

strains.name: Saccharomycodes ludwigii (species)
strains.description: A maltose- and maltotriose-negative yeast that leaves most wort sugars unfermented — used to make low- and non-alcoholic beer with a fermented aroma but minimal alcohol.
